Top 350 Brightspace Outreach

Now showing job titles directly on each card and inside the Outlook copy line.
#1Ready to send

Adrienne E Nordman

Assistant Vice President Of Member Relations And Education
Tennessee Hospital Association · Tennessee
Email: anordman@tha.com
Why them: Education / learning remit appears relevant for a Brightspace fit-check.
Likely pain: delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use
Cold email
Hi Adrienne,

I’m reaching out because other hospital & health care teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can help structure regulated education programs and make progress and completion easier to track.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Adrienne, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use. We’re seeing other hospital & health care teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Adrienne E Nordman is Assistant Vice President Of Member Relations And Education at Tennessee Hospital Association.

Beth Smull

Director Of Continuing Education Instruction
International Institute For Restorative PracticesGraduate School · PA
Email: bethsmull@iirp.edu
Why them: Credentialing / ce remit appears relevant for a Brightspace fit-check.
Likely pain: keeping CE or certification programs easy for learners while preserving clean reporting and governance
Cold email
Hi Beth,

I’m reaching out because other higher education teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is keeping CE or certification programs easy for learners while preserving clean reporting and governance.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can support repeatable certification and CE journeys with stronger visibility for the team running them.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Davoren Chick

American College Of Physicians · PA
Email: dchick@acponline.org
Why them: Education / learning remit appears relevant for a Brightspace fit-check.
Likely pain: delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use
Cold email
Hi Davoren,

I’m reaching out because other nonprofit organization management teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can help structure regulated education programs and make progress and completion easier to track.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Davoren, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use. We’re seeing other nonprofit organization management teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Davoren Chick is Senior Vice President| Medical Education at American College Of Physicians.

Likely pain: delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use
Cold email
Hi Beth,

I’m reaching out because other nonprofit organization management teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can help structure regulated education programs and make progress and completion easier to track.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Beth, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use. We’re seeing other nonprofit organization management teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Beth Lentchner is Senior Director Of Leadership Programs And Education at Tennessee Medical Association.

I’m reaching out because other hospital & health care teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can help structure regulated education programs and make progress and completion easier to track.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Ernest, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use. We’re seeing other hospital & health care teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Ernest Wang is Assistant Dean For Medical Education at NorthShore University Healthsystem.

Hi Victoria,

I’m reaching out because other medical devices teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is growing external training while keeping the learner experience polished and the delivery model manageable.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can support customer-facing learning programs, cohorts, and cleaner visibility into program progress.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

The pattern I’m curious about here is delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can help structure regulated education programs and make progress and completion easier to track.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Mike, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use. We’re seeing other hospital & health care teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Mike Abrams is President And CEO at The Ohio Hospital Association.

Hey Cindy, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use. We’re seeing other hospital & health care teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Cindy Case is Director Of Data Management at Wisconsin Hospital Association.

I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around delivering high-stakes education with reliable reporting, auditability, and a learner experience clinicians will actually use. We’re seeing other hospital & health care teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: James Kennedy is President at CDIMD.

We’re seeing other nonprofit organization management teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Michael Jacobs is President And CEO at Pennsylvania Health Care Association.

Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Why them: Education / learning remit appears relevant for a Brightspace fit-check.

Cold email
Hi Colleen,

I’m reaching out because other information technology and services teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can support association learning programs, CE delivery, and the operational reporting that sits around them.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Follow-up call script

I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches. I’m reaching out because other education management teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can support association learning programs, CE delivery, and the operational reporting that sits around them.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Cari, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches. We’re seeing other education management teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Cari Bushinski is Director Of Professional Learning at Michigan Association Of Superintendents & Administrators.

I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around turning member education into a stronger engagement and retention lever without adding more manual overhead. We’re seeing other financial services teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Paul Andrews is Vice President| International Advocacy And Engagement at World Council Of Credit Unions.

I’m reaching out because other government relations teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can support association learning programs, CE delivery, and the operational reporting that sits around them.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Bill, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches. We’re seeing other government relations teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Bill Rayl is Executive Director Of Workforce Solutions at Michigan Manufacturers Association.

The pattern I’m curious about here is scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can support association learning programs, CE delivery, and the operational reporting that sits around them.

Even if there’s no fit, I’d be happy to share a quick peek at what similar teams are doing and learn how you’re approaching it.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Annette, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around scaling member education, credentialing, or non-dues learning revenue without creating reporting headaches. We’re seeing other education management teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Annette Hofmann is Great American Insurance Group Chair at University Of Cincinnati Carl H. Lindner College Of Business.

I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around making onboarding, compliance, or skills development more manageable across teams. We’re seeing other consumer goods teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Jeff Lefebvre is Chief Human Resources Officer at Unimacts Global.

I’m reaching out because other museums and institutions teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is whether learning, member education, or training is enough of a priority to justify a real conversation.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: use a fit-check opener first and validate whether there is a meaningful learning motion before positioning Brightspace.

Even if there’s no fit, I can share a quick peek at what we’re seeing from similar teams — including no close match found.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?

Pat
Follow-up call script
Hey Bridget, it’s Pat from D2L. I sent over a note and figured I’d try you live for 30 seconds. I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around whether learning, member education, or training is enough of a priority to justify a real conversation. We’re seeing other museums and institutions teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Bridget Hovell is Director Of Membership at Carnegie Museums Of Pittsburgh.

I’m not calling with a dramatic ‘drop everything’ pitch — I’m mostly curious how your team is thinking about learning right now, especially around whether learning, member education, or training is enough of a priority to justify a real conversation. We’re seeing other higher education teams try to make learning easier to manage, easier to prove, and more useful to the people taking it. Would it be useful to compare notes, even just to give you a peek at what similar organizations are doing?

Context to keep in mind: Manasi Kekan is Adjunct Faculty at Thomas Jefferson University.

President
The Pathwayz Group · Oklahoma
Email: amber@thepathwayzgroup.com
Why them: General leadership remit appears relevant for a Brightspace fit-check.
Likely pain: making onboarding, compliance, or skills development more manageable across teams
Cold email
Hi Amber,

I’m reaching out because other human resources teams are trying to make learning feel less like admin overhead and more like something people actually use, value, and can report on. The pattern I’m curious about here is making onboarding, compliance, or skills development more manageable across teams.

At D2L, we usually help with the practical side of this: brightspace can organize learning paths and give leadership clearer visibility without making the first conversation a platform pitch.

Even if there’s no fit, I can share a quick peek at what we’re seeing from similar teams — including no close match found.

Worth a short compare-notes conversation?
